# Data Contracts
Trust in data
![Data Contract Diagram](https://datacontract.com/assets/datacontract-diagram-D0HFuI7O.png)
## What is a Data Contract?
A data contract is a document that defines the ownership, structure, semantics, quality, and terms of use for exchanging data between a data producer and their consumers. Think of an API, but for data.
## Open Data Contract Standard
The industry standard for data contracts is the [Open Data Contract Standard](https://bitol-io.github.io/open-data-contract-standard/latest/) (ODCS), led by Bitol (named after a mayan god, pronounced: bee-tohl), a Linux Foundation project. We joined forces with Bitol, contributed concepts and learnings, and with the release of ODCS 3.1, we can finally deprecate the [Data Contract Specification](https://datacontract-specification.com) proposal in favor of a common single standard.
Let's build an ODCS data contract step-by-step:
### Fundamentals
Every data contract starts with its fundamentals: the version of the standard, unique identifier, name, data contract version number, and status. This metadata identifies the contract and enables versioning.
### Schema
The schema defines the structure and semantics of your data. Here we define the table structure with its columns. Each property includes technical details, business semantics, and governance attributes.
### Data Quality
Data quality rules ensure that data meets expectations. Define checks like valid value constraints, row count thresholds, and custom SQL validation logic. These can be tested automatically with the Data Contract CLI.
### Team
Document who owns and maintains the data contract. Include team name, description, and members with their roles. Add support channels, how data consumers can reach out to the owners.
### Terms of Use
Define the purpose, usage guidelines, and limitations for your data. This helps consumers understand what they can and cannot do with the data, establishing clear governance boundaries.
### SLAs
Service Level Agreements define non-functional guarantees. Data consumers can match these with their use-case requirements.
### Servers
Finally, specify where the data lives. The server configuration includes connection details for different environments.
### Full Example
Here is the full data contract bringing all the elements together: fundamentals, a schema with two tables, data quality rules, team, terms of use, servers, and custom properties.
Reference: [Open Data Contract Standard](https://bitol-io.github.io/open-data-contract-standard/latest/)
orders-v1.odcs.yaml

```




apiVersion: v3.1.0




kind: DataContract




id: orders




name: Orders




version: 1.0.0




status: active




```

## Why Data Contracts Matter
Data contracts are not just documentation. They are the foundation for building trust and data governance with a high degree of automation.
  1. **Communication** : Bring data producers and data consumers together. Work through the data contract sections to capture domain knowledge and specify consumer expectations. Start contract-first using the visual [Data Contract Editor](https://github.com/datacontract/datacontract-editor) or the [Data Contract Excel Template](https://github.com/datacontract/open-data-contract-standard-excel-template).
  2. **Trust** : Automatically check that the data conforms to the data contract. Integrate the open-source [Data Contract CLI](https://github.com/datacontract/datacontract-cli) into your CI/CD or data pipelines to enforce data quality and contract compliance.
  3. **Discovery** : Data contracts provide the best possible metadata. Make them available in a central data marketplace, such as [Entropy Data](https://entropy-data.com), for potential data consumers to explore, request, and use. With [MCP](https://entropy-data.com/learn/mcp), AI agents can leverage the data contract metadata as necessary context to chat and work with business data.


Data Contracts are powerful when exchanging data with other teams or AI agents.
## Data Contract Editor
To create data contracts, we recommend using the [Data Contract Editor](https://editor.datacontract.com/) ([GitHub](https://github.com/datacontract/datacontract-editor)) which provides a visual interface for creating and editing contracts and comes with a live preview as a HTML representation.
Try out,
it's interactive!
[editor.datacontract.com](https://editor.datacontract.com)

# Fundamentals
Information about the data contract Name
    Orders Version
    1.0.0 ID
    orders Status
    active Contract Created
    1/15/2025, 5:00:00 AM Tags
    
e-commercetransactionspii
# Terms of Use
High level description of the dataset including purpose, usage guidelines, and limitations
[businessDefinition](https://entropy-data.com/policies/gdpr-compliance "GDPR compliance policy for handling customer data") Purpose
    
Provides order and line item data for analytics and reporting Usage
    
Used by analytics team for sales analysis and business intelligence Limitations
    
Contains only the last 2 years of data
Custom Properties
sensitivity
secret
# Schema
Schema supports both a business representation and physical implementation  
|  orders All historic web shop orders since 2020-01-01. Includes successful and cancelled orders. Quality Check  |  
| --- |  
|  Internal Order ID  
order_id  |  string UUID  |  The internal order id for every orders. Do not show this to a customer. Example: 99e8bb10-3785-4634-9664-8dc79eb69d43 primaryKeyrequireduniqueinternal  |  
|  Customer Number  
customer_id  |  string TEXT  |  A reference to the customer number Example: c123456789 requiredinternalcritical data elementpii:true  |  
|  Order Amount  
order_total  |  integer INTEGER  |  The order total amount in cents, including tax, after discounts. Includes shipping costs. Example: 9999 requiredQuality Check  |  
|  Order Date  
order_timestamp  |  timestamp TIMESTAMPTZ  |  The time including timezone when the order payment was successfully confirmed. Example: 2025-03-01 14:30:00+01  |  
|  Status  
order_status  |  string TEXT  |  The business status of the order Example: shipped Quality Check  |  
|  line_items Details for each item in an order  |  
| --- |  
| line_item_id  |  string UUID  |  Unique identifier for the line item Example: 12c9ba21-0c44-4e29-ba72-b8fd01c1be30 primaryKeyrequireduuid  |  
|  Stock Keeping Unit  
sku  |  string TEXT  |  Identifier for the purchased product Example: 111222333 required  |  
| price  |  integer INTEGER  |  Price in cents for this line item including tax Example: 9999 required  |  
| order_id  |  string UUID  | No description  |  
# Servers
This section covers connection details to physical data sets and infrastructure

    availability Value

    Data platform uptime guarantee

    retention Value
    1 Unit
    year Description
    Data will be deleted after 1 year
  * Property
    freshness Value
    24 Unit
    hours Description

  * Property
    support Value
    business hours Description
    Support only during business hours

dataPlatformRole

    role_orders_v1
Open the Data Contract Editor [in a new window](https://editor.datacontract.com), run it locally as [a standalone application](https://github.com/datacontract/datacontract-editor), or deploy it as a [Docker container](https://hub.docker.com/r/datacontract/editor) in your own environment. The Data Contract Editor is also included in [Entropy Data](https://entropy-data.com).
## Data Contract CLI
The [Data Contract CLI](https://github.com/datacontract/datacontract-cli) is an open-source command-line tool (and Python library and API server) that allows you to enforce data contracts and to detect schema drift. This is essential to build trust in your data product and ensure that the metadata is kept up-to-date.
Data Contract CLI

```


$uv tool install --python python3.11 'datacontract-cli[all]'


$export DATACONTRACT_POSTGRES_USERNAME=datacontract_cli.egzhawjonpfweuutedfy


$export DATACONTRACT_POSTGRES_PASSWORD=jio10JuQfDfl9JCCPdaCCpuZ1YO


$datacontract test https://datacontract.com/orders-v1.odcs.yaml



Testing orders-v1.odcs.yaml

🟢 data contract is valid. Run 25 checks. Took 1.615702 seconds.

```

The [Data Contract CLI](https://github.com/datacontract/datacontract-cli) supports all major data platforms, including Databricks, Snowflake, AWS, BigQuery, and Azure. Engineers can generate code and integrate with other systems using the _import_ and _export_ command. And the Data Contract CLI can also be used in Python scripts, notebooks, as a [GitHub Action](https://github.com/datacontract/datacontract-action/), as a [web server](https://api.datacontract.com/), and much more.
## Entropy Data
[Entropy Data](https://entropy-data.com) is our commercial product to manage data products with data contracts. It natively supports the Open Data Contract Standard, includes the Data Contract Editor, and integrates the Data Contract CLI.
[ entropy-data.com ![Entropy Data Marketplace](https://entropy-data.com/media/datamesh-manager-marketplace.webp) ](https://demo.entropy-data.com/?ref=datacontract-com-preview)
The core features are:
  * **Marketplace** : A self-service data product marketplace for teams and AI agents to discover, request, and access data products with automated approval workflows.
  * **Studio** : Design and develop data products using Web UI, YAML editor, API, or Excel templates with built-in testing to ensure compliance before production.
  * **Governance** : Define ownership rules, naming conventions, and data classification standards with AI-powered automated compliance checks.
  * **AI-Ready** : Model Context Protocol (MCP) enables AI agents to access data products and write queries in natural language.
  * **Flexible Deployment** : Available as cloud SaaS, self-hosted, or enterprise single-tenant options.


Try the [1-Click Playground](https://demo.entropy-data.com/?ref=datacontract-com-playground) or run the free [Community Edition](https://github.com/entropy-data/entropy-data-ce) locally on your machine with Docker.
## About Us
This website is maintained by [**Jochen**](https://www.linkedin.com/in/jochenchrist/) and [**Simon**](https://www.linkedin.com/in/simonharrer/). We want to spread the word about data contracts and data products, because we believe that high-quality data are the foundation effective decision-making, reliable AI, and long-term business impact. Our mission is to bring software engineering best practices and principles into the world of data, making data products as reliable and well-defined as APIs.
Our data journey started with [Data Mesh Architecture](https://datamesh-architecture.com), where we published our perspective on data products and data mesh. As software engineers and consultants, we helped enterprises adopt data mesh principles and recognized the need for better tooling around data products. We created the [Data Contract CLI](https://github.com/datacontract/datacontract-cli) as an open-source tool and initiated the [Data Contract Specification](https://www.datacontract-specification.com) to bring API-like contracts to the world of data.
Today, we are members of the Technical Steering Committee of [Bitol](https://bitol.io), a Linux Foundation project that maintains the [Open Data Contract Standard](https://bitol-io.github.io/open-data-contract-standard/latest/) and the [Open Data Product Standard](https://bitol-io.github.io/open-data-product-standard/latest/).
We believe in open standards. That's why we created the Data Contract Specification based on OpenAPI principles, and later deprecated it to bring our ideas into the Open Data Contract Standard, because a standard supported by many is more important than your own specification.
In [Entropy Data](https://entropy-data.com), our commercial product, we bring everything together: open standards, data contract tooling, and a platform to manage data products with data contracts in a beautiful user interface.
![Entropy Data Logo](https://entropy-data.com/media/logo_fuchsia_v2.svg)
